name: Data & Integration Control Plane

scenarios.title: Create a durable data integration control packet
steps: Open the Sprint 9 Data & Integration Control workspace.

Review API Hub connector readiness, ingestion run health, data contract gaps, mapping jobs/templates, staging batches/rows, assistant review items, master-data quality, twin ingest events, and action queue evidence.
expected: A durable AssistantDataIntegrationPacket row is created with source summary, connector readiness, data contract, mapping review, staging review, master-data quality, twin ingest, launch checklist, response plan, rollback plan, and leadership summary JSON.
An AssistantAuditEvent records that packet creation is evidence-only.
No connector, credential, mapping template, staging row, master-data record, twin ingest event, partner access, sync job, or downstream source record is changed automatically.

scenarios.title: Queue integration launch review
steps: Add an optional integration launch review note to a draft Sprint 9 packet.
expected: The packet moves to INTEGRATION_REVIEW_QUEUED and stores actionQueueItemId.
An AssistantActionQueueItem is created for data_integration_launch_review with the packet URL and readiness evidence.

Review remains separate from rollout; the assistant does not activate connectors, change credentials, apply staging rows, mutate master data, append twin ingest events, expose partner access, or trigger sync execution automatically.

scenarios.title: Approve a reviewed Sprint 9 packet
steps: Review the packet leadership summary, connector readiness, contracts, mapping gaps, staging risks, MDM posture, twin ingest evidence, launch checklist, and rollback controls.
expected: The packet status becomes APPROVED with approvedAt, approvedByUserId, and approvalNote.
An AssistantAuditEvent records approval and reiterates the no-silent-mutation guardrail.
Approval marks the integration readiness packet as reviewed only; execution still requires separate downstream approved API Hub, MDM, twin, security, partner-access, or sync workflows.

guardrails: No API Hub connector is activated, paused, edited, deleted, or synced automatically.
No credential or auth configuration is changed or exposed automatically.
No mapping template, mapping analysis job, staging batch, or staging row is edited, promoted, discarded, applied, or deleted automatically.
No product, supplier, customer, location, or integration master data is merged, overwritten, enriched, deleted, or published automatically.
No twin ingest event is appended and no twin graph record is upserted automatically.
No partner access is created or exposed automatically.
No downstream ERP, WMS, TMS, finance, API Hub, or source-system record is mutated automatically.
